The '''negative''' mood in Aelaki is formed through an '''/f/-infix''' negation system, which inserts the consonant ''f'' into the root template.

== Overview ==

Negation in Aelaki is expressed morphologically rather than syntactically. Instead of a separate negative particle, the /f/ consonant is infixed directly into the word's root template.

== Template ==

The negative infix follows the pattern:

: <code>C₁V₁fC₂V₂C₃</code>

The ''f'' is inserted between the first vowel and the second consonant of the root.

== Restrictions ==

The /f/-infix negation system has specific restrictions:

* It '''never''' appears in the yes/no particles ''Su'' and ''Fu''.
* It is restricted to '''content words''' and '''linking particles''' such as ''zuf'' ("and not").

== Application ==

The negative infix applies across multiple parts of speech:

* '''Verbs''': Standard verbal negation
* '''[[Adjective]]s''': e.g., ''koran'' "bright" → ''korfan'' "not bright"
* '''[[Adverb]]s''': e.g., ''zadate'' "lengthy" → ''zafdafte'' "not lengthy"

See also the [[Positive]] mood, which is the unmarked counterpart.

[[Category:adjective things]]
[[Category:git synced pages]]
